title: How Much Revision Should Be Included in Web Design?
excerpt: Revisions are an inevitable and healthy part of any web design project. But how many rounds should be included as standard? What counts as a revision vs. a new request? This post gives you the answers so you can set expectations clearly from the start.
content:
Revisions are a normal, expected part of any web design project. No first draft is perfect — and good designers build a revision process into their workflow. But unlimited revisions aren't realistic, and poorly managed revisions can derail timelines and inflate costs.

Here's what you need to know.

What's Standard in the Industry?

Most professional web design packages include 2–3 rounds of revisions per major design phase. A "phase" typically means:

  • Wireframe revision rounds (1–2 rounds)

  • Visual design revision rounds (2–3 rounds)

  • Development revision rounds (1 round for content/copy adjustments)


Some agencies use a single consolidated revision round after each major milestone. What matters is that revision expectations are documented in the contract.

What Counts as a Revision?

A revision typically means:

  • Adjustments to layout, spacing, color, or typography based on feedback

  • Rewriting or reorganizing copy within agreed content areas

  • Swapping out images or icons

  • Minor additions or removals of content elements


A revision does NOT typically mean:
  • Adding new pages not in the original scope

  • Completely changing the design concept after approval

  • Adding new features or functionality

  • Reverting to a previously rejected direction


These are scope changes, and they usually attract additional charges.

How to Give Effective Revision Feedback

Poor feedback leads to inefficient revisions. Instead of "I don't like this," try:

  • "The headline feels too generic — can we make it more specific to our audience?"

  • "The color here feels cold — can we try a warmer version of the brand palette?"

  • "The CTA button isn't standing out enough — can we make it larger and use the accent color?"


Specific, actionable feedback saves time for everyone.

What Happens When You Run Out of Rounds?

Most agencies charge for additional revision rounds beyond what's included in the contract. Rates vary but are typically quoted as hourly or as a flat additional round fee.

We Build Revision Time Into Every Project

At Bluewaves Technology, our project proposals clearly define revision rounds per phase — so there are never any surprises. We guide you through the feedback process to ensure revisions are productive and efficient.
